extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won Madrid circuit sprint, perfectly positioned by RICHEZE after Bennett left final acceleration too late inside 100m.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck Quick-Step's Richeze forced between rival leadout riders mid-road to deliver Jakobsen, overwhelming pure sprinter speed.
Bennett's positioning behind Jakobsen wheel was perfect but his final acceleration timing 100m out proved fatally late.
Multiple sprint trains (UAE, Sunweb, CCC) massed but Astana inexplicably rode front without sprinter, wasting energy.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: second stage win suggests he handles Madrid circuit technical finish better than pure power sprinters
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: wheel-surfing execution perfect but hesitated on final kick, revealing possible confidence deficit against Quick-Step train
rider_signals.CAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: UAE organized leadout but Gaviria invisible in finale, suggesting form collapse late in Grand Tour
